reason: The remediation involves modifying kernel command line arguments via GRUB or bootc. While this is a standard hardening practice, changes to the bootloader/kernel arguments carry an inherent risk of preventing the system from booting correctly if the syntax is malformed or if the specific kernel version has compatibility issues with the argument.
estimated_impact: reboot required; Potential for boot failure if GRUB configuration is corrupted; Kernel parameter incompatibility; Requires reboot to take effect
